pageTitle: SAI International School UX Case Study: Simplifying the Admissions Journey

sections.title: SAI International School Admissions
sections.subtitle: Transforming a confusing, lengthy form into a clear, multi-step process that builds user confidence.

visual.altText: A visual representation of a simplified, multi-step online form on a desktop screen.

sections.title: Impact by the Numbers
sections.heading: By reducing cognitive load and clarifying the path forward, we saw immediate improvements in form completion rates and user feedback.

projectDescription.label: SAI International School
projectDescription.description: A top-tier educational institution facing high drop-off rates on a critical conversion path: the online application form.

projectDescription.label: Project Scope
projectDescription.value: Admissions UX Redesign. The challenge was to transform a single, overwhelming form into an intuitive, multi-step wizard, while clarifying the post-submission journey.

issues.name: Complex Form
issues.description: The admission form was presented as one long page, causing 'form fatigue' and high abandonment rates before submission.

issues.name: Post-Submission Confusion
issues.description: The main pain point identified through research: users were confused about the steps after filling the form (interviews, documents, follow-up).

issues.name: Lack of Process Visibility
issues.description: No visual indicator or textual guide existed to explain the overall admissions lifecycle.

sections.title: The Client’s Vision: Clarity and Guidance

visual.altText: A progress bar showing a user on step 2 of 4 of a process.

goals.name: Reduce Form Anxiety
goals.description: Break down the application into manageable chunks to make the task feel less daunting and increase completion rates.

goals.name: Clarify the Entire Journey
goals.description: Provide a simple, step-by-step map of the entire admissions process, not just the online form submission.

goals.name: Build User Confidence
goals.description: Use guiding UX copy and predictable design to assure users that they are on the right track at all times.

steps.name: User Research & Pain Point Mapping
steps.description: Conducted user interviews and analyzed existing data to pinpoint the true pain points, revealing that confusion after the form was a bigger issue than filling the form itself.

steps.name: Information Architecture (IA) Re-structuring
steps.description: Organized the form fields into logical, themed steps (e.g., 'Parent Details,' 'Student Details,' 'Course Selection').

steps.name: Multi-Step Form Design
steps.description: Implemented a multi-step design with a persistent progress indicator, visually illustrating how far the user is from completion.

steps.name: Process Clarification Flow
steps.description: Designed a dedicated section that clearly explains the full post-application process (e.g., Screening, Interview, Selection, Admission).

solutions.title: Multi-Step Form Implementation
solutions.description: Replaced the monolithic form with a series of smaller, focused pages. This design leverages the Zeigarnik effect to encourage completion by showing progress.

solutions.title: Visible Progress and Guiding Copy
solutions.description: Added a top-of-page progress bar and used concise, encouraging UX copy at the start of each step (e.g., 'You're halfway there!').

solutions.title: Clear 'What Happens Next' Flow
solutions.description: Crucially, a dedicated, visually distinct section was added to transparently outline all steps following the form submission, eliminating the anxiety of the unknown.

solutions.title: In-Line Help and Tooltips
solutions.description: Added contextual help text and instructional microcopy next to complex fields to prevent hesitation and reduce input errors.

takeaways.title: Manageable Chunks Boost Completion
takeaways.description: Splitting a large task into smaller, labeled steps significantly reduces perceived effort. It's a key tactic for increasing completion rates on high-friction activities like application forms.

takeaways.title: The Post-Task Journey is Just as Critical
takeaways.description: The research proved that user anxiety often peaks after the primary interaction (form submission). Clarifying the subsequent steps is essential UX design for managing expectations and reducing support load.

takeaways.title: User Research Finds the Hidden Problem
takeaways.description: Focusing solely on the form itself would have missed the primary pain point. User research was crucial in revealing the true source of confusion lay in the lack of process transparency.

limitations.name: Project Timeline
limitations.description: The project carried a firm deadline, pushing the team to stay aligned and deliver on time.

limitations.name: Phase 1
limitations.description: Initial planning and research took place, establishing the framework for the project.

limitations.name: Phase 2
limitations.description: Development and implementation, where the core features were built and tested.

limitations.name: Phase 3
limitations.description: Final review and adjustments, ensuring everything met the quality standards before the launch.
